summary: Decomposed, multi-criteria metric design for LLM pipelines. The metric IS the optimization target: decompose holistic judges into orthogonal yes/no sub-judges, return bool during compile and float during eval, bake length in deterministically, use a cross-family judge, mitigate judge bias, and human-calibrate (>=20 spot-checks) before any compile. Source basis: dspy-sop Case C + ops op-004/op-024/op-030; llamaindex-sop OP-10 evaluator triad; judge-bias research [arxiv.org/pdf/2506.02592, arxiv.org/pdf/2509.26072].

operations.name: DecomposeMultiCriteria
operations.trigger: Open-ended generation; multiple correctness axes (factuality + tone + length + relevance).
operations.action: List 3-6 orthogonal yes/no sub-criteria. One dspy.Predict(Assess) call per criterion. Aggregate with AND (hard gates) + weighted sum (soft preferences).
operations.output: Multi-judge metric callable; one criterion per call.
operations.evidence: [dspy.ai/learn/evaluation/metrics/]; dspy-sop Case C step 2; LlamaIndex Faithfulness+Relevancy+Retriever triad.

operations.name: BoolDuringCompileFloatDuringEval
operations.trigger: Writing any metric used for both compile (teleprompt.compile) and dspy.Evaluate.
operations.action: Inside the metric, if trace is not None: return bool_aggregate; else: return float_aggregate.
operations.output: One metric function, two modes; optimizer avoids chasing float noise.
operations.evidence: [dspy.ai/learn/evaluation/metrics/]; [dspy.ai/cheatsheet/]; dspy-sop op-030 (cited as OP-030 in SKILL.md).

operations.name: ExplicitLengthPenalty
operations.action: Add a deterministic scalar length_score = clip(1 - max(0, len - budget) / budget, 0, 1) multiplied into the final float. Do NOT delegate length judgment to the LLM.
operations.output: Length-controlled metric immune to the judge's length bias.
operations.evidence: [arxiv.org/pdf/2506.02592]; dspy-sop Case C step 6 ('Add a length penalty as a separate scalar').

operations.name: CrossFamilyJudge
operations.trigger: Judge model is in the same family as the task model (e.g., both GPT-4).
operations.action: Swap the judge to a different family (Claude, Llama, Gemini, Mistral). Document the choice.

operations.name: HumanSpotCheck20
operations.trigger: Before any compile / production rollout of a new or modified metric.
operations.action: 20 (open-ended: 30) human ratings on the same sub-criteria. Compute per-sub-judge and aggregate agreement. Reject if <80% per-sub or aggregate; fix the metric before compiling.
operations.evidence: dspy-sop Case C step 4 ('Spot-check the metric on 20 examples ... If sub-judges disagree with human on >20% of cases, fix the metric before compiling'); [dspy.ai/learn/evaluation/metrics/]. NOTE: '30 if open-ended' and the >=80% bar are SKILL.md tightenings of the DSPy '20 / >20%' source.

operations.name: RAGFaithfulnessRelevancyContext
operations.trigger: RAG pipeline; tuning chunk size / reranker / retriever-k / hybrid alpha.
operations.action: Instantiate three evaluators: FaithfulnessEvaluator (answer entailed by retrieved context), RelevancyEvaluator (answer addresses query), RetrieverEvaluator(['mrr','hit_rate']) (does retrieval pull the gold passage). Gate every PR.
operations.output: 3-axis RAG quality vector; never a single number.
operations.evidence: LlamaIndex OP-10 EvalLoop ('FaithfulnessEvaluator + RelevancyEvaluator + RetrieverEvaluator(["mrr","hit_rate"]). Gate every change'); [developers.llamaindex.ai/python/framework-api-reference/evaluation/].

operations.name: BiasProbeSuite
operations.action: Run length-probe, self-preference probe, position probe, rubric-order probe (Stage 4) on a 20-example dev set. Log deltas.
operations.output: Bias-probe report; rejected if length probe >=+5pp on padded answers or position flips >10%.
operations.evidence: [arxiv.org/pdf/2509.26072]; [arxiv.org/pdf/2506.02592]. Probe thresholds (+5pp, >10%) are SKILL.md author-chosen operationalizations.

operations.name: TextualFeedbackForGEPA
operations.trigger: Sub-judge can articulate why it failed (e.g., 'answer was verbose', 'missed cited source').
operations.action: Return dspy.Prediction(score=float, feedback=str) from the metric. Pair with dspy.GEPA optimizer (not MIPROv2).
operations.evidence: [dspy.ai/api/optimizers/GEPA/overview/]; [arxiv.org/abs/2507.19457]; dspy-sop op-004 (cited as OP-004 in SKILL.md); Case C step 5 (10-13% lift over MIPROv2 on AIME-style benchmarks).

operations.name: RandomizeJudgeOrder
operations.trigger: Pairwise judge or rubric with N>3 criteria.
operations.action: Per-call: randomize A/B order in pairwise; randomize sub-judge ordering. Or run both and average.

operations.name: CalibrationReceipt
operations.action: Save JSON next to the artifact: {metric_version, n_spot_checks, per_judge_agreement, bias_probe_results, judge_model_id, task_model_id, date, criteria_list}.
operations.output: Audit-ready provenance for the metric used to compile.
operations.evidence: General audit best-practice; DSPy save(save_program=True) analog [dspy.ai/tutorials/saving/]. Receipt schema is SKILL.md author-defined.

research_caveats: DSPy operation IDs are cited upper-case in SKILL.md (OP-030, OP-024, OP-004) but are lower-case in the dspy-sop source (op-030, op-024, op-004). Operations map 1:1; only casing differs.
The >=80% agreement threshold and '30 if open-ended' spot-check count tighten DSPy's documented '20 examples / >20% disagreement' bar.
Bias-probe numeric thresholds (+5pp length, >10% position flip, >5pp rubric-order) are author-chosen operationalizations, not figures from the cited bias papers.
Dilemma 3 (cekura-metric-design, '11 installs') and all numeric outcomes inside the three Dilemmas are illustrative scenario figures, not measured results.
